zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Coney於1519年出生在英國林可爾史爾吉爾頓時，她的父親Thomas Coney35歲，母親Agnes  Princess of Coney born Oldenburg34歲。她於大約1538年與William Perry I在英國林可爾史爾吉爾頓結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1568年12月31日在她的家鄉享年49歲。

nameMeanings.name: Coney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English cony ‘rabbit’ (a back-formation from conies, from Old French conis, plural of conil), perhaps for someone thought to resemble a rabbit in some way or who wore rabbit skin or who sold rabbits or their skins. One York trader with this name in 1323 was a peleter or furrier.

variants.name: Cunning
meanings: Irish and Scottish (Fife): variant of Scottish or English Conning, from Middle English, Anglo-Norman French coning ‘rabbit’, used for someone thought to resemble a rabbit in some way; perhaps also an occupational name for a furrier. See also Coney .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Coney was born in 1519, in Kirton, Lincolnshire, England, her father, Thomas Coney, was 35 and her mother, Agnes  Princess of Coney born Oldenburg, was 34. She married William Perry I about 1538, in Kirton, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 31 December 1568, in her hometown, at the age of 49.

Elle a épousé William Perry I environ 1538, à Kirton, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 31 décembre 1568, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 49 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Coney(이)가 1519년 영국 링코실르 커턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Coney(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes  Princess of Coney born Oldenburg(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 대략 1538년 영국 링코실르 커턴에서 William Perry I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1568년 12월 31일 그녀의 고향에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Coney родилась в 1519 г. в местности киртон, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Coney было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes  Princess of Coney born Oldenburg, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Perry I около 1538 г. в местности киртон, линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 31 декабря 1568 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 49 лет.
